title: Spring Hike — Ravenswood Park
description: A beautiful loop through coastal forest. We'll stop midway for coffee and a chance to talk. No experience needed — just bring water and good shoes.

photos.caption: Rocky forest trail
photos.desc: The classic Ravenswood trail winding through moss-covered boulders

photos.caption: Glacial erratic
photos.desc: A massive glacial erratic deposited here thousands of years ago

photos.caption: Fall foliage from Ledge Hill
photos.desc: Views stretch out to Gloucester Harbor and beyond on clear days

photos.caption: Boulder field
photos.desc: One of New England's most striking glacial landscapes

photos.caption: Autumn foliage
photos.desc: Ravenswood is spectacular in fall — reds, oranges, and gold through 600 acres

photos.caption: Cathedral forest
photos.desc: Towering pines and hemlocks create a cathedral-like canopy

photos.caption: Rock outcroppings
photos.desc: Granite ledges offer natural rest spots and views

photos.caption: The trail ahead
photos.desc: Well-marked trails make Ravenswood accessible for all skill levels

photos.caption: Ravenswood Park
photos.desc: 481 Western Ave (Route 127), Gloucester — 600 acres managed by The Trustees

photos.caption: Along the path
photos.desc: A comfortable loop through the heart of the park

photos.caption: Forest floor
photos.desc: Rich woodland ecology — ferns, lichen-covered boulders, and birdsong

title: Morning Fishing — Salem Willows
description: Early morning fishing off the pier. Gear available for those who need it. We'll grab breakfast after. This is as relaxed as it gets.

photos.caption: Salem Willows
photos.desc: A historic seaside park with sweeping views of Salem Harbor

photos.caption: Sunrise over the water
photos.desc: Early morning light on Salem Harbor — worth the 7am start

photos.caption: Sailboats in the harbor
photos.desc: Salem Harbor on a calm morning

photos.caption: Spring blooms
photos.desc: The park is beautiful in May — wildflowers along the waterfront

photos.caption: The willows
photos.desc: The park's iconic weeping willows that give it its name

photos.caption: Waterfront view
photos.desc: Looking out over the harbor from the fishing pier

photos.caption: Aerial view
photos.desc: Salem Willows from above — the peninsula jutting into Salem Harbor

photos.caption: The pier
photos.desc: Where we'll set up and fish for the morning

photos.caption: Park grounds
photos.desc: Plenty of space to spread out, relax, and enjoy the morning

photos.caption: Morning light
photos.desc: Salem Willows is magical in the early hours

title: Kayak Launch — Marblehead Harbor
description: A guided paddle around the harbor. Kayaks provided. Relaxed pace with plenty of stops. No experience needed.
